page: Microsoft Azure M128-32ms is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the memory-intensive MS family. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon 8280L cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The defining characteristic of this server is its massive memory capacity of 3892.0 GB, yielding an exceptionally high resource density of 121.62 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, the instance includes 4194 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear trade-off between single-core processing power and memory throughput. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU test (1038.39) and Geekbench single-core score (900.0), fall into the weak and poor categories respectively, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also performing poorly. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations perform significantly better. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (21882.64) and Geekbench multi-core score (7790.0) are average. Notably, the large block memory write bandwidth is strong at 135.69 GB/sec, while database operations and Redis SET operations (21.11 million operations/sec) maintain steady average-tier performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M128-32ms represents a specialized hardware profile where the budget is heavily allocated toward extreme memory density rather than raw CPU compute speed. This makes it a highly specialized option for workloads that require massive datasets to reside entirely in memory. It is ideally suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise relational database management systems, heavy caching layers, and memory-bound data processing pipelines. Conversely, it is poorly suited for CPU-bound single-threaded tasks or large language model inference, where text generation speeds are benchmarked as poor.
description: The Microsoft Azure M128-32ms is a highly specialized, memory-optimized server from the MS family, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8280L processor with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz, it features an extraordinary 3892.0 GB of RAM, translating to 121.62 GB per core. The server is equipped with 4194 GB of local storage and supports premium storage, but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates a trade-off: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression are weak, whereas multi-core operations, database tasks, and large-block memory write bandwidth (135.69 GB/sec) deliver solid average-to-strong results. This hardware profile is qualitatively optimized for memory-bound applications rather than raw compute power, making it a precise fit for large in-memory databases, extensive caching layers, and heavy data processing workloads that demand massive memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M128-32ms is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 3892.0 GB of RAM, and 4194 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive enterprise database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M128-32ms is a memory-optimized server with 32 Intel vCPUs, 3.8 TB of RAM, and 4.1 TB of local storage for large database workloads.
tagline: An ultra-high-density memory instance featuring massive RAM capacity and local storage for memory-bound enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8280L processors at 2.7 GHz
Extremely high memory capacity of 3892.0 GB (121.62 GB per core)
Includes 4194 GB of local storage with premium storage support
Strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 135.69 GB/sec
Best suited for large in-memory databases and heavy caching layers
Lower single-core CPU performance makes it less ideal for compute-heavy tasks
